message:
fix(storage): clear close_reason and closed_by_session on issue reopen

When `update_issue` detects a transition from "closed" → "open"
(via `issue.closed_at.is_some() && updates.closed_at.is_none()`),
it cleared `closed_at` but left `close_reason` and
`closed_by_session` populated from the previous close. That left
the row in an inconsistent state — the issue was reportedly open,
but a CSV/JSON export, the changelog generator, or any downstream
report that joined on those fields would still attribute it to the
prior close session and reason.

Treat the reopen as a full close-state reset:
  - `issue.close_reason = None;`
  - `issue.closed_by_session = None;`
  - emit the corresponding `add_update("close_reason", "")` and
    `add_update("closed_by_session", "")` so the SQL UPDATE
    actually clears the columns (matching how the `closed_at`
    clear is sent as `SqliteValue::Null`).

The existing `deleted_at` reset block immediately below is
unchanged — that one already cleared everything it needed to.

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust pass.
